_meta.note: Update this file weekly. Max 5 cards. Each card should represent a structural shift, not a news headline.

signals.title: House control is three seats away
signals.context: Democrats need a net gain of just 3 seats to flip the House. Republicans hold a 220–215 majority — the thinnest margin in decades.

signals.title: 12 Senate seats are in play
signals.context: Ballotpedia identifies 12 battleground Senate seats this cycle. Republicans are defending one seat in a state Harris won — Maine.

signals.title: Democrats flipped two governorships
signals.context: Spanberger in Virginia and Sherrill in New Jersey both won by wider-than-expected margins, signaling early headwinds for Republicans.

signals.title: A record wave of retirements
signals.context: Over 56 sitting members of Congress — more than 10% — are not running for reelection. 25 are Republicans, often a leading indicator of a coming wave.

signals.title: Congressional maps are being redrawn mid-cycle
signals.context: Texas, North Carolina, Missouri, and Ohio have redrawn district maps between elections — a rare move that could affect dozens of competitive races.
